messages.content: 帮我润色In this study, Fan first mentioned the use of photoacoustic imaging to monitor the dynamics of aluminum adjuvants in real time to track the space-time transport in organisms. Aluminum adjuvants were successfully labeled with BSA@ICG complex utilized a two-step process, and when BSA@ICG@Alum combining OVA, they showed improved immune response compared to OVA alone. Photoacoustic imaging (PAI) was used to quantitatively visualize aluminum adjuvant diffusion through lymphatic vessels. However, fluorescence imaging failed to detect metastases. Although the idea of this study is innovative, unfortunately, the current data do not provide sufficient support.

In this study, Fan introduced the utilization of photoacoustic imaging to monitor the real-time dynamics of aluminum adjuvants for tracking their space-time transport in organisms. A two-step process successfully labeled the aluminum adjuvants with BSA@ICG complex. Additionally, when BSA@ICG@Alum was combined with OVA, an improved immune response was observed compared to OVA alone. Photoacoustic imaging (PAI) was employed to visualize the diffusion of aluminum adjuvants through lymphatic vessels in a quantitative manner. However, metastases were not detected using fluorescence imaging. While the study's concept is innovative, the current data lacks sufficient support.

帮我润色In this study, Fan first mentioned the use of photoacoustic imaging to monitor the dynamics of aluminum adjuvants in real time to track the space-time transport in organisms. Aluminum adjuvants were labeled with BSA@ICG complex utilized a two-step process, and when BSA@ICG@Alum combining OVA, they showed improved immune response compared to OVA alone. Photoacoustic imaging (PAI) was used to quantitatively visualize aluminum adjuvant diffusion through lymphatic vessels. However, fluorescence imaging failed to detect metastases. Although the idea of this study is innovative, unfortunately, the current data do not provide sufficient support. Specific reasons are as follows.

In this study, Fan proposed the use of photoacoustic imaging to monitor the real-time dynamics of aluminum adjuvants and track their transportation within organisms. The aluminum adjuvants were successfully labeled using a two-step process with the BSA@ICG complex. Furthermore, when combined with OVA, BSA@ICG@Alum showed an enhanced immune response compared to OVA alone. To quantitatively visualize the diffusion of aluminum adjuvants through lymphatic vessels, photoacoustic imaging (PAI) was employed. However, despite these advancements, fluorescence imaging failed to detect any metastases. Although the study's concept is innovative, the current data lacks sufficient support. The following are specific reasons for this inadequacy.

帮我润色1.	In the labeling process of aluminum adjuvant with BSA@ICG complex, the zeta potential of both are negative values, how can they combined with each other tightly through electrostatic absorption effect? Moreover, the zeta potential of formed BSA@ICG@Alum was less than minus 10, which means particles are unstable and tend to condense. In other PAI related work (Acta Biomaterialia, 2019, 99, 363-372; ACS Appl. Mater. Interfaces 2017, 9, 46, 40017–40030), the zeta potential of particles loaded with BSA and ICG is very large, which means that they have good stability

1. In the process of labeling aluminum adjuvant with the BSA@ICG complex, both the aluminum adjuvant and BSA@ICG complex have negative zeta potentials. This raises concerns about how they can effectively combine through electrostatic absorption, which typically requires opposite charges to attract. Additionally, the zeta potential of the resulting BSA@ICG@Alum complex was found to be less than minus 10, indicating that the particles are unstable and prone to aggregation. In other studies related to photoacoustic imaging (PAI) (such as Acta Biomaterialia, 2019, 99, 363-372; ACS Appl. Mater. Interfaces 2017, 9, 46, 40017–40030), the particles loaded with BSA and ICG exhibited a significantly higher zeta potential, implying better stability.

帮我润色1.	In the labeling process of aluminum adjuvant with BSA@ICG complex, the zeta potential of both are negative values, how can they combined with each other tightly through electrostatic absorption effect? Moreover, the zeta potential of formed BSA@ICG@Alum was less than minus 10, which means particles are unstable and tend to condense. In other PAI related work (Acta Biomaterialia, 2019, 99, 363-372; ACS Appl. Mater. Interfaces 2017, 9, 46, 40017–40030), the zeta potential of particles loaded with BSA and ICG is very large, which means that they have good stability

1. In the labeling process of aluminum adjuvant with the BSA@ICG complex, a potential concern arises due to the negative zeta potential values of both the aluminum adjuvant and the BSA@ICG complex. Typically, electrostatic absorption requires opposite charges for effective binding. This raises questions about how these two negatively charged entities can tightly combine with each other. Moreover, it is worth noting that the zeta potential of the resulting BSA@ICG@Alum complex was found to be less than minus 10, suggesting that the particles are unstable and prone to aggregation. 

In contrast, other studies related to photoacoustic imaging (PAI), such as those published in Acta Biomaterialia, 2019, 99, 363-372 and ACS Appl. Mater. Interfaces 2017, 9, 46, 40017–40030, have demonstrated large zeta potential values for particles loaded with BSA and ICG. These higher zeta potentials indicate improved stability, further contrasting with the findings of the current study.

帮我润色The PA imaging In vitro provided in this paper does not show that BSA@ICG@Alum signal is stronger than ICG, and PA imaging has higher resolution and deeper penetration thickness than fluorescence imaging.

The PA imaging conducted in vitro in this paper does not provide evidence that the signal from BSA@ICG@Alum is stronger compared to ICG alone. It is important to note that PA imaging offers higher resolution and deeper penetration depth compared to fluorescence imaging. These characteristics make PA imaging a valuable tool for visualizing and tracking the dynamics of aluminum adjuvants in organisms.

帮我润色The PA imaging In vitro provided in this paper does not show that BSA@ICG@Alum signal is stronger than ICG, it seems that ICG signal is stronger. Furthermore, it does not show that PA imaging has higher resolution and deeper penetration thickness than fluorescence imaging. When the depth is more than 6mm, there is minimal detection in both two imageings.

The PA imaging conducted in vitro in this paper does not demonstrate that the signal from BSA@ICG@Alum is stronger compared to ICG alone. On the contrary, it appears that the signal from ICG is stronger. Additionally, the paper does not provide evidence supporting the claim that PA imaging has higher resolution and deeper penetration depth than fluorescence imaging. In fact, both imaging techniques show minimal detection when the depth exceeds 6 mm.

帮我润色In vivo images of Antigen reservoir effect, the fluorescence map (Figure 4d) and the data map (Figure 4e) were inconsistent. The standard error of mean fluorescent intensity of the three groups in Figure 4e is too large. It seems that there is no statistical difference in the amount of antigens at 0h,12h,24h,48h and 72h. It is not possible to conclude that BSA@ICG@Alum has a longer residence time at the injection portion

In the in vivo images of the Antigen reservoir effect, there is inconsistency between the fluorescence map (Figure 4d) and the data map (Figure 4e). Additionally, the standard error of mean fluorescent intensity for the three groups shown in Figure 4e is quite large. These observations suggest that there may not be a statistically significant difference in the amount of antigens present at 0h, 12h, 24h, 48h, and 72h. Therefore, it is not possible to conclude that BSA@ICG@Alum has a longer residence time at the injection site based on the available data.

帮我润色PAI images of different parts of mice are not intuitive and persuasive. Please refer to other literature for data presentation of similar results, such as J. Nanobiotechnology. 2020; 18: 49; J. Mater. Chem. B, 2023,11, 2157-2165.

The PAI images obtained from different parts of the mice in this study may not be visually intuitive or persuasive. To strengthen the data presentation and draw more convincing conclusions, it would be beneficial to refer to other literature that provides examples of similar results. For instance, J. Nanobiotechnology. 2020; 18: 49 and J. Mater. Chem. B, 2023, 11, 2157-2165 could serve as references, as they may offer alternative methods of data presentation for comparable findings. Incorporating such literature can enhance the overall robustness and credibility of the study.

帮我润色5. There are many small mistakes in the article, such as:

a. Wrong uppercase In figure 3's note, and wrong lowercase alum in figure 5b's picture

b. Figure 5c The format shown in the data diagram is inconsistent with the format written in the article "OVA-Alum", "OVA-BSA@ICG@Alum".

c. AX,IN these abbreviations are not given their full names when first mentioned in the article

d. There are blank pages in the middle of the article

e. The language needs to be polished to make it more logical

5. The article contains several minor mistakes, including:

a. Incorrect capitalization in the note of Figure 3 and incorrect lowercase usage for "alum" in Figure 5b's picture.

b. Inconsistency in the format shown in Figure 5c's data diagram compared to the format mentioned in the article ("OVA-Alum" vs. "OVA-BSA@ICG@Alum").

c. The full names of abbreviations such as AX and IN are not provided when first mentioned in the article.

d. Blank pages are present in the middle of the article.

e. The language requires further polishing to enhance logical flow.

Addressing these issues will improve the accuracy, consistency, and readability of the article.
